Tier-4 script name: v7-docs HOW-TO-RUN says 'tier4.py'; verified via ls — only mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier4_run.py exists (plus tier4.json). v7-engine-code is correct; the stale name originates in docs/v7/STRATEGY.md §8 itself.

Band daily-boundary timezone: v7-docs says the band check runs 'once per UTC day at 00:00 rollover'; verified in mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/sim.py — first_share_trigger uses resample('D')/daily close on the bar index, which is tz-naive BROKER SERVER time (UTC+2/+3, hour 0 = 17:00 ET) per the IC feed schema. The boundary is server midnight, not UTC midnight. Any FMA3 reimplementation must use the server-time calendar day or it will diverge from both the Python anchor and the EA.

Python 'source of truth' vocabulary: v7-docs anoints the R8 honest tick book (89.7%/15.70%/19.44%, gbandrebal engine) as validation source of truth; v7-research-trail anoints the R10 close-mark panel (band_sim on h15/base_matrix.parquet, 108.50%/18.84%/€956,197/nreb 42) as the mandatory reproduction baseline for post-V7.0 studies. Both are true in scope but FMA3 must not conflate them: three distinct Python baselines coexist (R8 battery book, R10 recon reference with 27 triggers, R10 selection panel) plus the MT5 tick run. Pin which one any new study reproduces.

Minor, non-blocking: mag_xau active fraction 17% (v34-docs) vs ~18–22% (v34-code) — two measurement recipes, like the officially dual-cited 1.463/1.25 standalone Sharpe; and v3.4 pin n_trades 20,403 (1m engine fills) vs trade_chars 13,709 (hourly FIFO upper-bound) — different counters, both documented, not defects.
gaps: GATE-SET ACCOUNTING IS CROSS-ENGINE AND CURRENTLY UNDEFINED FOR FMA3: the campaign targets mix engines. CAGR>96.1 / DD<20.9 / Sharpe>2.03 / crisis tail<=35.6 are MT5 real-tick R10 numbers; '0 neg years AND <=1/24 neg quarters' is only satisfied by v3.4's Python 1m close-to-close M2M convention (V7.0 itself scores 3/24 negQ on the MT5 tick, and 0/24 only on the misleading Python close-mark panel). No existing engine produced all six goal numbers simultaneously. FMA3 must pre-register ONE engine+accounting for its gates and recompute both baselines in it (candidates: FMA2-style 1m worst-mark engine baselines = 88.66/21.67 for v3.4 and needs a fresh NSF5-book run; or MT5 real-tick = 96.1/20.9 for v7.0 and needs a first-ever FMA2 tick run). Without this, 'beats both books' is unfalsifiable.

MT5 path for the merged book: the 96.1% headline can only be reproduced in the MT5 Strategy Tester (cannot be run from Python), FMA2 has NEVER had an MT5 real-tick run (reconciliation verdict PARTIAL by construction), and no reader documented the MT5 terminal/Wine setup, how to obtain the tester environment, or the file locations of the R-frontier reports _32–_37 (only the hardcoded Wine path in build_ic_feed.py:16 hints MT5 exists locally). If MT5 is the deployable arbiter for FMA3, this is unbudgeted work: a new EA covering FMA2-style sleeves plus a reconciliation harness.

Sleeve-interface bridge is unspecified: NSF5 sleeves emit per-1m-bar NOTIONAL-multiple target arrays sized on slot equity (band premium REQUIRES slot-equity sizing; flips −7.31pp under fixed-notional per H8); FMA2 sleeves emit hourly fraction-of-joint-equity matrices in a fixed-fraction single account (band ruled NOT IMPORTABLE by their v3.0 RECON, and FMA2 sleeves invert under band cadence: crisis −20.9pp per H15). Both cross-import channels are formally EXHAUSTED/banned from re-litigation. The intelligence map contains no licensed merge design: FMA3 must state which architectural family it is (band/slot-equity vs fixed-fraction/hard-caps, or a two-account federation) and which pre-registered tests are permitted, before any sleeve is ported — including the mandatory fixed-schedule rebalance ablation for any band-book seat change, and the rule that the FMA2 'intraday' sleeve and NSF5 F1 are the same edge (ρ0.87, must not carry both).

2026 data asymmetry blocks crash-inclusive validation on the FMA2-style engine: MT5 headline includes 2026-06; Python 1m holdout (NSF5 cache/bars_1m_holdout) ends 2026-04-30 for 12 symbols (EURUSD/XAUUSD 2026-05-31) and is Dukascopy-UTC format; FMA2 execution-grade exec bars end 2025-12-31 with NO 2026 execution-grade build; USTEC (a core sleeve) has NO Dukascopy feed at all (2026H1 uses a USA500 proxy, corr .89). A Feb-2026-crash-inclusive merged-book validation needs a data build that no reader scoped.

Operational gaps, minor: no requirements.txt/pyproject in either repo (env pinned only informally: python 3.13.12, numba 0.64.0, pandas 2.3.3, numpy 2.4.2, pyarrow 23.0.1); FMA2 builder scripts hardcode absolute NSF5 paths and NSF5's tier12.py/engine_check.py hardcode the NSF5 repo path — FableMultiAssets3 (currently empty) needs a copy/symlink/patch plan for the 4.6 GB cache before any run; validate_v1_4.py reads a stale v1.2 curve and must not be reused for FMA3 breach numbers.

engine_of_record_verdict: UNAMBIGUOUS. (A) The v7.0 gate quintet — CAGR 96.1% / MaxDD 20.9% / Sharpe 2.03 / crisis tail 35.6% (plus PF 1.67, ML 190%, 0/6 negY, 3/24 negQ) — was produced by the MT5 REAL-TICK Strategy Tester running mt5/ea/PortfolioV7.mq5 with preset V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set at R=10, IC Markets EU Raw, €10k hedging account, 2020-01→2026-06 crash-inclusive (R-frontier reports _32–_37). 20.9% is MT5's 'Maximal' equity DD (€-worst, ~R-invariant) and 35.6% is MT5's 'Relative' DD (%-worst, the Mar-2020 COVID event) — two different events reported first-class. All three v7 readers agree; verified consistent with DASHBOARD.html embedded data (tickMaxDD −20.9 / tickRelDD −35.6). Not to be conflated with the two Python engines: the R8 honest band book (gbandrebal sim.run_generic = v51_bandharvest._run_window; 89.72%/15.70% bd/19.44% tick/Sharpe 2.58/€532,230/31 triggers — byte-verified in engine_reproduce.json; this is the run on which the 7/7 hard-gate battery passed) and the R10 close-mark selection panel (band_sim; 108.50%/18.84%/€956,197 — reproduction baseline for post-V7.0 studies, understates the crisis tail ~0.53× and shows 0 negQ). (B) The v3.4 numbers — CAGR 88.66% / MaxDD 21.67% worst-mark / Sharpe 1.8543 / 0 negY / 1 negQ (2023Q1 −1.42%) / €449,708 / breach 0.1208 — were produced by the Python 1-MINUTE single cross-margined account engine research/account_engine_1m.py::simulate_account_1m (NOT core.py; v34-docs misattributed the file), invoked by research/eval_v34_pin_s10.py on the book built as combine(sleeves, RAW weights)×GLOBAL_SCALE 10 + apply_hard_limits, pinned in research/outputs/v34_s10_pin_1m.json (config_hash 48c09199fbf83d82) — verified by reading the JSON. v3.4 has NO MT5 real-tick run. Consequence for FMA3: the campaign's six gate numbers straddle these two non-comparable engines and accounting conventions (MT5 tick counts V7.0 at 3/24 negQ; the <=1/24 target is a 1m-engine close-to-close convention) — pin one engine of record and recompute both baselines in it before any experiment.
